examples.text: اِنْخَفَضَتْ نِسْبَةُ ٱلْبِطَالَةِ فِي ٱلسَّنَوَاتِ ٱلْأَخِيرَةِ.
examples.english: The unemployment rate has decreased in recent years.

examples.text: تَدْرُسُ نِسْبَةٌ كَبِيرَةٌ مِنَ ٱلشَّبَابِ فِي ٱلْخَارِجِ.
examples.english: A big percentage of young people study abroad.

senses.gloss: nisba, a common suffix to form adjectives of relation or pertinence, e.g. ـي (-ī, -iyy) m and ـيَة (-íyya) f.
in proportion to, in comparison with
in allusion to, after (as in "named after")

examples.text: جَامِعُ الفَاتِحُ سُمَّى نِسْبَةً لِلسُّلْطَانِ مُحَمَّدِ الفَاتِحِ
examples.english: Fatih Mosque was named after the sultan Mehmed the Conqueror.

etymology.text:
Verbal noun of نَسَبَ (nasaba).

Adverbial accusative of نِسْبَة (nisba, “relationship”).
